---
title: '`syncweaver contribute`'
---

Contribute a patch that was created in a host repository back to the upstream
source repository as a pull request.
The command clones the source, applies the patch to a new branch, pushes it,
and opens a PR — all without leaving the host repository directory.

## Usage

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from ccbr_tools.shell import shell_run

help_text = shell_run("syncweaver contribute --help")
print("```")
print(help_text)
print("```")
```

## Examples

```sh
# Contribute the patch for a tracked path (lockfile auto-resolved)
syncweaver contribute --path code/mypackage

# Specify source repo and patch file explicitly
syncweaver contribute \
  --path code/mypackage \
  --source-repository OWNER/mypackage \
  --patch patches/mypackage.patch \
  --base-ref main

# Pass a token directly (or set GITHUB_TOKEN in env)
syncweaver contribute \
  --path code/mypackage \
  --token ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxx
```
